key_insight: SFBench introduces a de novo, expert-annotated benchmark for judging the scientific feasibility of materials-science claims, with open-ended explanations and five-point feasibility scores.
engineering_relevance: It gives engineers and AI researchers a realistic testbed for systems that must assess whether proposed scientific ideas are plausible before investing in experiments or model development.
method: Expert ground-truth feasibility ratings on a 5-point scale
Open-ended explanations plus baseline evaluation with recent GPT models
evidence_signal: The benchmark uses 197 claims with subject-matter-expert labels and explanations, enabling evaluation of both score prediction and explanation quality.
watch_item: Because the task is open-ended and feasibility judgments are subjective, performance may depend strongly on rubric consistency and expert alignment.
paper_title: SFBench: The SciFy Scientific Feasibility Benchmark
